steps.title: Identify what s(t) represents
steps.content: The problem states that $s(t) = 120(0.5)^{\frac{t}{31}}$ gives the approximate mass, in grams, that REMAINS $t$ years after the 120 gram sample starts to decay. The key word is remains — so $s(t)$ is the remaining mass, not the amount lost.

steps.title: Interpret s(62) = 30 using the definition
steps.content:
Since $s(t)$ represents the remaining mass, $s(62) = 30$ means: at time $t = 62$ years, the remaining mass is 30 grams.

Check each choice:
  • A: Approximately 30 grams of the sample remains 62 years after the sample starts to decay — remaining mass = 30g, time = 62 years ✓
  • B: mass decreased by approximately 30 grams — this would mean 120 - 30 = 90g remaining, not 30g ✗
  • C: describes 30 years (wrong time) ✗
  • D: 62 grams remains after 30 years (wrong values) ✗
That matches Choice A — Approximately 30 grams of the sample remains 62 years after the sample starts to decay..
